examples.title: Velocity (Speed + Direction)
examples.content: Speed is scalar: 60 km/h. Velocity is vector: 60 km/h east. Same number, but direction makes it a vector.

examples.title: Force Direction Matters
examples.content: Push with 50 N upward = object lifts. Push with 50 N downward = object gets pressed down. Same magnitude, opposite directions, opposite results!

examples.title: Displacement vs Distance
examples.content: Walk 5 m north then 5 m south. Distance (scalar) = 10 m. Displacement (vector) = 0 m (you're back where you started). Direction changes everything!
